summary: PR 2694 added a local --fix mode to tools/check, with dry-run unified diff support, and made only the jobj-flags check auto-fix raw hidden-flag literals to JOBJ_HIDDEN. assert-macros remained detection-only because the PR body and comments say that conversion requires judgement. Tests were added for jobj flag fixing behavior, including preserving surrounding text, non-match safety, and idempotence.

key_files.role: Adds Check.fix support, --fix/--dry-run CLI handling, jobj-flags fixer, refactors scanning helpers, and keeps assert-macros detection-only.
evidence_refs: diff: Check gains fix: FixFn | None
diff: fix_jobj_flags replaces spans with JOBJ_HIDDEN
diff: run_fix writes files or prints difflib.unified_diff for dry-run
pr_body: Only jobj-flags is auto-fixed; assert-macros stays detection-only

key_files.role: Adds unit tests for fix_jobj_flags replacement forms, exact text preservation, non-match behavior, and idempotence.

what_changed: tools/check documentation and usage now describe --fix and --fix --dry-run.
A FixFn type and optional Check.fix field were added so checks can remain scan-only or provide an auto-fixer.
jobj-flags was given fix_jobj_flags, which finds raw hidden-flag literal argument spans in HSD_JObjSet/ClearFlags calls and replaces only the literal token with JOBJ_HIDDEN.
assert-macros scanning was refactored into helper functions but intentionally left without a fixer.
The driver was split into run_checks and run_fix; run_fix applies enabled fixers, writes files unless dry-run, prints diffs in dry-run, and reports remaining findings.
Tests were added for all literal forms, exact surrounding text preservation, non-matches, and idempotence.

smart_moves.text: The PR restricted auto-fixing to the exact mechanical jobj-flags replacement and explicitly left assert macro conversion for manual review.
evidence_refs: pr_body: assert-macros stays detection-only; converting it needs judgement
diff: Check('assert-macros', ...) has no fix argument

smart_moves.text: The fixer uses spans from strip_noise while preserving offsets, then applies replacements in reverse order to avoid invalidating later spans.
evidence_refs: diff: for lit_start, lit_end in sorted(spans, reverse=True)

smart_moves.text: The test suite covers safety properties important for automated source rewriting: untouched non-matches, exact surrounding text preservation, and idempotence.
Dry-run mode previews a unified diff and writes nothing, which makes automated cleanup reviewable before modifying the tree.
evidence_refs: pr_body: --fix --dry-run prints a unified diff and writes nothing
diff: difflib.unified_diff in run_fix when dry_run

decomp_lessons.lesson: Automated cleanup is safest when limited to exact, token-only decomp style fixes; judgement-heavy rewrites should remain detection-only or manual.
evidence_refs: pr_body: raw hidden-flag literal replaced with JOBJ_HIDDEN; purely mechanical and exact
diff: fixes change only tokens, not layout

decomp_lessons.lesson: For raw JObj hidden flag literals, the intended cleanup target in this PR is JOBJ_HIDDEN, but only when the hidden flag appears as the complete argument to supported HSD_JObj flag calls.
evidence_refs: diff: _HIDDEN_LITERAL regex accepts 16, 0x10, octal-like 020 forms with suffixes

decomp_lessons.lesson: Assert macro conversion may require negating an if-guard and checking text equivalence; this PR treats that as not safely auto-fixable.
evidence_refs: diff comment: Detection only; if-guard must be negated and matched against assert text

naming_conventions.convention: tools/check check identifiers use kebab-case such as jobj-flags and assert-macros; CLI disables are derived as --no-<check>.
evidence_refs: diff: Check.name comment says kebab-case id drives --no-<name>

naming_conventions.convention: Scanner and fixer functions follow scan_<check> and fix_<check> naming, as shown by scan_jobj_flags and fix_jobj_flags.
Private parsing helpers in tools/check use leading underscores, e.g. _jobj_literal_spans, _assert_sites, _has_todo, and _assert_first_arg.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a project check/fixer for mechanical JObj hidden flag cleanup instead of hand-editing many raw 0x10 literal sites.
evidence_refs: pr_body: mechanical fix can be applied directly instead of by hand
pr_body: Replay reproduced #2675 across 73 files with zero content differences except clang-format line wrapping

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: After using the fixer, run clang-format because the PR claims fixes change only tokens, not layout.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Fixes change only tokens, not layout — run clang-format afterward
diff docstring: Fixes change only tokens, not layout, so run clang-format afterward

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use --fix --dry-run to inspect the exact unified diff before writing changes.
evidence_refs: pr_body: --fix --dry-run prints a unified diff and writes nothing

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Questioned the maintainability of procedural parsing and asked whether pure regex or parser combinators could be used.

review_feedback.author: itsgrimetime
review_feedback.feedback: Responded that regex likely cannot capture all assert cases in the project; mentioned considering clang-query and parser combinators, with parser combinators implying an extra Python dependency.

review_feedback.author: itsgrimetime
review_feedback.feedback: Tested clang-query and clang-tidy locally and asked whether checks should live alongside tools/issues or as standalone clang-query matchers in CI.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Approved but stated the main issue was that the implementation seemed annoying to maintain as written and preferred one of the alternatives discussed earlier.

evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl for changed paths and line counts
review_comments.md and text_corpus.jsonl for review discussion
notes: The loaded PR evidence directly includes PR body, changed files, full diff, and review comments.
The PR body claims 15 unit tests and replay on the pre-sweep tree; intake records this as PR-author-provided verification, not independently rerun validation.
Review comments mention clang-query/clang-tidy and parser combinators as alternatives considered, but the evidence does not prove a final project-wide tooling direction beyond this PR.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep tools/check auto-fixers limited to exact mechanical rewrites
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2694 supports a tools/check pattern where a check may expose an optional fixer, but only exact token-level rewrites are auto-fixed; judgement-heavy conversions remain reported for manual review.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Only jobj-flags is auto-fixed; assert-macros stays detection-only
diff: assert-macros Check has no fixer

accepted_candidate_records.title: Automated fixer tests should cover exactness and idempotence
accepted_candidate_records.text: The jobj-flags fixer tests added in PR 2694 cover multiple literal spellings, preservation of surrounding text, non-match safety, and idempotence.

source_update_candidates.title: tools/check/main.py supports --fix and --dry-run for jobj-flags only
source_update_candidates.text: As of PR 2694, tools/check/main.py has a --fix mode and --fix --dry-run mode. Only the jobj-flags check has an auto-fixer in this PR; assert-macros remains detection-only and reports remaining findings for manual review.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-level fact for future tooling work; should be curated into source-owned path facts if still current.

source_update_candidates.title: Raw JObj hidden flag literals should become JOBJ_HIDDEN when the argument is exactly the hidden flag
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2694 treats replacing a raw hidden-flag literal argument in HSD_JObj Set/Clear Flags calls with JOBJ_HIDDEN as an exact mechanical cleanup. Non-exact expressions such as combined flags are intentionally left untouched by the tests.
evidence_refs: pr_body: raw hidden-flag literal is replaced with JOBJ_HIDDEN
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible decomp style standard, but curator should verify against current headers and existing standards before promoting.

source_update_candidates.title: Do not auto-fix direct __assert to HSD assert macros without semantic review
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2694 documents that assert-macros conversion is judgement-laden because the if-guard must be negated and matched against assert text, and not every site is byte-identical; the tool reports those sites rather than rewriting them.
evidence_refs: diff comment above assert macro scanner
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential refinement of assert/report cleanup guidance; should be reconciled with existing assert-report-macros standard.

rejection_notes: Do not infer from this PR that all tools/check parsing should move to clang-query, clang-tidy, regex, or parser combinators; review comments only discuss alternatives and limitations.
Do not treat the author-provided replay claim as independently verified by this intake; no build or replay was run here.
Do not generalize the jobj-flags fixer to combined flag expressions; the added tests explicitly leave 0x10 | JOBJ_PTCL untouched.
